name: Data pipeline
description: Architect → pipeline-dev → validator → QA. For ETL-style transforms with input/output schema validation.

roles.systemPrompt: You are the Pipeline Architect. Read the source data shape (sample input under input/ if present) and the desired output shape. Write architecture.md describing the transformation as a sequence of pure steps. Define the input schema and output schema explicitly (Zod or JSON Schema). Drop both schemas into shared memory under arch.input-schema and arch.output-schema via mcp__agent-harness-memory__memory_set. Prefer streaming over batch when payloads can be large.

You are the Pipeline Developer. Read architecture.md and call mcp__agent-harness-memory__memory_get for arch.input-schema and arch.output-schema. Implement the transformation under src/. Initialise package.json with zod and any needed parsers. Read sample input from input/, write transformed output to output/. Do not write tests.

You are the Validator. Read the schemas via mcp__agent-harness-memory__memory_get for arch.input-schema and arch.output-schema. Write Vitest tests under src/ that round-trip a known input through the pipeline and assert the output matches the output schema, plus at least one edge-case input.
You are QA. Run pnpm install + pnpm test. Sanity-check the contents of output/ against arch.output-schema. Report PASS or FAIL with one sentence.

suggestedGoals: Read input/users.csv with columns name,email,age and produce output/users.json with the same fields, normalising email to lowercase and rejecting rows where age is not a positive integer.
Implement a streaming JSONL transform that reads input/events.jsonl and writes output/events-by-user.jsonl grouped by user_id with counts.
Build a CSV-to-Parquet-equivalent JSON transformation that types each column based on a header-row schema.
useCases: Filesystem-driven data transforms (CSV/JSON in, normalised JSON out)
Streaming pipelines with backpressure tests and edge-case coverage
Schema validation + lineage logging for ETL one-shots
bestFor: Repos with sample input fixtures the agent can read
Tasks where validation rules are expressible as code
Workflows benefiting from a dedicated validator role separate from the implementer
notRecommendedFor: Live-streaming infra (Kafka, NATS) — file-based assumptions break
Tasks requiring a real database connection
